title: Daughter of Frankenstein protocol for nuclei isolation from fresh and frozen tissues using OptiPrep® continuous gradient
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7043l","text":"This protocol is the result of the combination of various nuclei isolation protocols for single cell RNA-seq experiments using droplet-based methods, and is an extension of the Frankenstein (D.O.F means Daughter Of Frankenstein). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c0ba7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a15j5","text":"Developed to prepare nuclei isolates from fresh and frozen material of small-to-large sizes. The good thing is that it does not uses FACS but OptiPrep® continuous gradient to remove debris. It is the alternative protocol when FACS is not available. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":152,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":152,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e59f5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"80amc","text":"This protocol works better with smaller samples. After centrifugation the nuclei will form a pellet and contaminating membranes will float at the top. This continuous gradient can be applied for as many times as needed to the same prep to continue cleaning up your nuclei suspensions. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"77n6s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"96aa","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3j5dt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

Gradient Solutions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"en2f7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"efs6","text":"G60: OptiPrep®: 60% (w/v) solution of iodixanol in water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7i9q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4cq81","text":"GD: 150 mM KCl, 30 mM MgCl2, 120 mM, Tricine-KOH pH 7.8.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"agooa","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5rkqs","text":"GW: 50% of iodixanol --\u003e mix 5 volumes of G60 with 1 volume of GD (final: 50% iodixanol, 25 mM KCl, 5 mM MgCl2, 20 mM Tricine-KOH pH 7.8).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ggsm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"77jdv","text":"GH: 0.25 M Sucrose, 25 mM KCl, 5 mM MgCl2, 20 mM Tricine-KOH pH 7.8. Stock solutions and GD/GH Set-Up","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dghjf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7kqer","text":"-500 mM Tricine: 8.96 g in 100 ml water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lcj7","text":"-1 M KCl: 7.45 g in 100 ml water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d99gj","text":"-1 M MgCl2x6H2O: 20.3 g in 100 ml water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5rstv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8vcf9","text":"GD: To 50 ml water add 24 ml, 15 ml and 3 ml respectively of the Tricine, KCl and MgCl2•6H2O stock solutions (above); adjust to pH 7.8 with 1 M KOH and make up to 100 ml. Keep at RT.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"66f8h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"33jgp","text":"GH: Dissolve 8.5 g of sucrose in 50 ml of water; add 4 ml, 2.5 ml and 0.5 ml respectively of the Tricine, KCl and MgCl2•6H2O  stock solutions (above); adjust to pH 7.8 with 1 M KOH and make up to 100 ml. Keep at 4°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dploh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aqev8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dhgci","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7394e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c57qp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"erpld","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"98hh8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":119307,"guid":"419820EB341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Nuclei EZ lysis buffer ","mol_weight":0,"sku":"EZ PREP NUC-101","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Mince/chop tissue with a razor blade to small pieces. The tissue may be as small as a grain of rice. For mincing the tissue, you may take the tube out of ice, however, be quick and return it to ice.
Add 300 µL of chilled Nuclei EZ Lysis Buffer (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL) to the tissue in 1.5 mL tube.
Gently homogenize the sample using a douncer by stroking 10-20 times. Keep nuclei suspension on ice at all times.

Add an extra 700 µL of chilled Nuclei EZ Lysis Buffer (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL), mix gently by pipetting using wide-bore tips and incubate on ice for 00:05:00 . Repeat mixing 2-3 times during the incubation.
Filter homogenate using a 70 μm-strainer mesh to fit a 15 ml Falcon tube (e.g. pluriStrainer Mini 70 μm Cell Strainer).
Transfer flow though into a 1.5 mL LoBind tube and centrifuge the nuclei at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C and remove supernatant leaving behind ~ 50 µL . Homogenate after filtering. Nuclei pellet.

Optional: add 1 mL of EZ Lysis buffer (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL), gently resuspend pellet (optional: incubate for 00:05:00 on ice for additional lysis). Then centrifuge the nuclei at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C . Homogenate after re-pelleting.
Remove supernatant as much as possible without disturbing pellet (if pellet looks loose leave ~ 50 µL behind) and add 300 µL of G30 buffer. Mix well but gently to resuspend the pellet.

Carefully, underlay the sample with 1 mL of the G30. A clear separation between homogenate and added G30 will form. Do not disturb. Homogenate after resuspending and underlaying G30. Arrow indicates the separation of both layers.

Centrifuge the nuclei at 8000 x g, °C, for 00:20:00 at 4 °C . After centrifugation the nuclei will form a pellet and contaminating membranes will float at the top. This continuous gradient can be applied for as many times as needed to the same prep to continue cleaning up your nuclei suspensions (Optional Step 11). Clean pelleted nuclei.
Optional: repea t steps 8-10) for additional cleaning.

After centrifugation, remove supernatant and add 1 mL of ice-cold Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer supplemented (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL) without resuspending the nuclei. Then centrifuge the nuclei at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .

Remove supernatant as much as possible without disturbing pellet (if pellet looks loose leave ~ 50 µL behind) and resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of ice-cold Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer supplemented (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL). Then centrifuge the nuclei at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .

Remove supernatant and resuspend with 300 µL of ice-cold Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL) and filter using 40-um Flowmi® Cell Strainer and transfer to a new LoBind 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.

Check integrity and purity under microscope and count manually or using an automatic counter. For automatic counter I recommend LUNA-FL™ Dual Fluorescence Cell Counter and Acridine Orange/Propidium Iodide (AO/PI) Cell Viability Kit (F23001). DAPI-stained nuclei post-clean up. BioA traces of total RNA rom HEK293 cell line and RNA extracted from a representative nuclei suspension; in this case liver as an example. This sample was successfully run on the 10x Genomics Chromium using the 3' v3.1 Dual Index kit.
Proceed to your amazing snRNA-Seq experiment!
creator: Luciano G Martelotto
